ucc.why_it_matters: The UCC is Western's student activity centre — the hub for student social, cultural, and recreational life on campus. It's packed with clubs, food, lounges, and non-stop OWeek programming all day.

concrete_beach.subtitle: Outdoor Plaza
concrete_beach.why_it_matters: The large open plaza in front of the UCC is the social heart of campus — host to clubs fairs, food trucks, live performances, and late-night hangouts throughout OWeek.

uc_hill.subtitle: South Campus Lawn
uc_hill.why_it_matters: The iconic sloped lawn in front of University College is one of Western's most recognizable gathering spots. It's a go-to for outdoor activities, lawn games, and spontaneous hangouts.

aceb.subtitle: Amit Chakma Engineering Building
aceb.why_it_matters: Western's newest engineering building is a practical and inspiring space where undergrads integrate classroom theory with hands-on learning. Home base for all Engineering faculty events this week.

health_sci.subtitle: Arthur & Sonia Labatt Health Sciences Building
health_sci.why_it_matters: The Health Sciences Building houses Western's health, rehab, and kinesiology programs. It's the gathering point for all Health Sciences and Science faculty home bases during OWeek.

talbot.subtitle: Don Wright Faculty of Music
talbot.why_it_matters: Talbot College is home to Western's Don Wright Faculty of Music and the Paul Davenport Theatre, which hosts over 200 performances a year. Music students gather here for their first-year welcome.

mckellar.why_it_matters: McKellar Theatre is a campus performance venue hosting screenings, film events, and cultural programming. Catch the OWeek movie night here tonight.

schmeichel.why_it_matters: An academic building on Western's campus supporting faculty teaching and research programs throughout the year.

somerville.why_it_matters: Somerville House is a charming academic building on Western's west campus, home to student commons and evening programming including the OWeek movie watch party.

thames.why_it_matters: Thames Hall supports Western's social science and humanities faculties with lecture halls and seminar rooms for everyday teaching and academic events.

rec_centre.subtitle: Western Student Recreation Centre
rec_centre.why_it_matters: The WSRC is Western's state-of-the-art recreation facility — featuring an 8-lane pool, full gyms, courts, and drop-in fitness classes running all week during OWeek.

alumni_stadium.why_it_matters: Western Alumni Stadium is home to the Mustangs football and field hockey teams and sits at the heart of campus athletics. Tonight it hosts the OWeek Film on the Field and Global Village.

kings.why_it_matters: King's is Western's Catholic liberal-arts college — the largest affiliated college at Western, with its own campus, residences, and tight-knit community. King's students meet here for their OWeek home base.

huron_quad.subtitle: Huron University College
huron_quad.why_it_matters: Huron Quad is the grassy heart of Huron University College, surrounded by historic buildings and used for outdoor student events throughout the year.

bayfield.why_it_matters: Bayfield Hall is one of Western's student residences, offering a community-focused living experience close to campus amenities and OWeek activities.

clare.why_it_matters: Clare Hall is a smaller, community-focused residence at Western known for its close-knit atmosphere. Clare hosts its own community night during OWeek.

delaware.why_it_matters: Delaware Hall is one of Western's classic residences with a welcoming community and a large main lounge — the site of tonight's Delaware social.

elgin.why_it_matters: Elgin Hall is a lively residence with a backyard gathering space perfect for outdoor socials — including tonight's Elgin Backyard Social during OWeek.

essex.why_it_matters: Essex Hall is a mid-campus residence with its own cafeteria and a strong floor community. Essex hosts free food and social events for residents throughout OWeek.

lambton.why_it_matters: Lambton Hall is a quiet, community-oriented residence at Western with a main lounge perfect for floor social nights throughout OWeek.

medsyd.subtitle: Medway-Sydenham Hall
medsyd.why_it_matters: Med-Syd is one of Western's larger residence complexes, home to a big student community and lively outdoor quad events during OWeek.

ontario.why_it_matters: Ontario Hall is a traditional Western residence with a spacious courtyard used for community socials and OWeek outdoor programming.

perth.why_it_matters: Perth Hall is one of Western's smaller residences, known for its tight-knit community and cozy dining hall — the gathering spot for tonight's Perth Social.

saugeen.why_it_matters: Saugeen-Maitland is Western's largest and most iconic residence, housing nearly 1,200 students. It's the social epicentre of first-year residence life and OWeek.
